Project.Name: Data Science Copilot
Project.Vision: Build a CedarOS + Mastra-powered multi-agent system where data scientists can upload datasets and receive automated cleaning, analysis, visualization, and modeling through specialized agents. The system emphasizes explainability, interactivity, and productivity, replacing hours of Jupyter notebook work with minutes of guided collaboration.

Workflow.Step: User uploads dataset (CSV, JSON, Excel, or API)
Backend stores dataset (DuckDB or Parquet optional)
User requests agent operation via frontend (Cleaner, Modeler, etc.)
Mastra orchestrator agent delegates task to specialized agent
Agent checks tools → runs code in Python sandbox if needed
React components auto-update with latest state
User reviews proposals in Agent Timeline (accept/decline)
Final results saved optionally in DuckDB/Parquet

Agent.name: Orchestrator
Agent.role: Directs workflow, delegates tasks, manages sequencing

Agent.name: Cleaner
Agent.role: Fix NaNs, type conversions, remove/add columns

Agent.name: Modeler
Agent.role: Build predictive models with scikit-learn, evaluate metrics

Agent.name: Explainer
Agent.role: Explain results to user, summarize agent outputs

Frontend.State: Global CedarOS State for datasetRows, summaryStats, charts
Frontend.UI: Unique multi-panel layout designed for data science workflows

Sandbox.Purpose: Run generated code safely (Pandas, NumPy, Scikit-learn, Plotly)

Phase.name: Commit 00
Phase.goal: Repo setup, CedarOS + Mastra stub chat

Phase.name: Final
Phase.goal: End-to-end working demo with all agents

GuidingPrinciples.Principle: User always sees proposals before acceptance
State is transparent and explainable via CedarOS
UI is designed around how data scientists think (not chatbots)
